blocks:
Surah Alam Nashrah

Meccan, its number of verses is eight verses by consensus (بالإجماع)* بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ***

(In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١)** أَلَمْ نَشْرَحْ لَكَ صَدْرَكَ ** (1) Did We not expand your breast for you?

It was said: Did We not make it spacious with knowledge (العلم) and wisdom (الحكمة), and the receiving of the revelation (الوحي), and patience upon harm (الأذى) and hardships (المكاره), until it encompassed the intimate discourse (مناجاة) with the Truth (الحق) and the call (دعوة) to creation (الخلق), so he was absent yet present.

Al-Qummi said: (It was expanded) by Ali (peace be upon him), so We made him your successor (وصيّك). He said: And when Mecca was conquered (فتح مكّة) and Quraysh entered Islam, Allah expanded his breast and His secret (سرّه).

And in al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet (Allah bless him and his progeny): It was said to him: For the breast to be expanded. He said: Yes. They said: O Messenger of Allah, is there a sign (علامة) by which it is known? He said: Yes, detachment (التجافي) from the abode of delusion (دار الغرور), and turning (الإنابة) towards the abode of eternity (دار الخلود), and preparation (الاعداد) for death before its descent.

(٢) وَوَضَعْنا عَنْكَ وِزْرَكَ ** (2) And relieved you of your burden, That which was heavy upon you to bear. Al-Qummi said: It is the burden (ثقل) of war. (٣) الَّذِي أَنْقَضَ ظَهْرَكَ ** (3) which weighed down your back? It was said: meaning, it weighed down your back until it caused it to creak (النقيض). And it is the sound of the man [or his back] due to the heaviness of the load. And it is a similitude, meaning that if it were a physical load, the creaking (نقيض) of his back would have been heard. (٤) وَرَفَعْنا لَكَ ذِكْرَكَ (4) And exalted your renown? Al-Qummi said: You are mentioned when He is mentioned. And this is the saying of the people: "I bear witness that there is no god but Allah, and that Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله." And in al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله regarding this Ayah, he said: Gabriel said to me: Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: "When I am mentioned, you are mentioned with Me."

(٥) فَإِنَّ مَعَ الْعُسْرِ ** (5) For indeed, ease follows hardship. (The hardship (الْعُسْرِ)) is like the constriction of the chest, the burden (الوزر) that breaks the back, the misguidance of the people, and their harm (إيذائهم). (The ease (يُسْراً)) is like the expansion of the chest (شرح الصدر), the removal of the burden (وضع الوزر), and the granting of success (توفيق) to the people for guidance (للاهتداء) and obedience (الطاعة). Therefore, do not despair of the comfort (روح) of Allah if He shows you that which grieves you. (٦) إِنَّ مَعَ الْعُسْرِ يُسْراً ** (6) Indeed, ease follows hardship. This is an affirmation (تأكيد) and a renewal (استئناف) with the promise of another ease (يسر آخر), such as the reward of the Hereafter (ثواب الآخرة). In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: He came out joyful and happy, laughing and saying: "One hardship (عسر) will never overcome two eases (يسرين)." ** فَإِنَّ مَعَ الْعُسْرِ يُسْراً إِنَّ مَعَ الْعُسْرِ يُسْراً ** (5) For indeed, ease follows hardship. (6) Indeed, ease follows hardship. It is said that the reason (الوجه) for this is that الْعُسْرِ is definite (معرّف), so it does not multiply (يتعدّد), whether it refers to a specific instance (للعهد) or the genus (الجنس). However, the ease (اليسر) is indefinite (منكّر), so the second instance is different from the first.

(7) فَإِذا فَرَغْتَ فَانْصَبْ. (7) So when you are done, appoint. (8) وَإِلى رَبِّكَ فَارْغَبْ (8) And turn to your Lord eagerly. It was said: It means that when you finish one act of worship, follow it immediately with another, connecting some of them to others, and do not leave any of your times empty, unoccupied by worship.

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir and al-Sadiq (peace be upon them): فَإِذا فَرَغْتَ ** (So when you are done) from the obligatory prayer (الصلاة المكتوبة), فَانْصَبْ ** (appoint) yourself to your Lord in supplication (الدعاء), and turn to Him eagerly in asking, and He will grant you.

And from al-Sadiq, narrated from him (peace be upon him): It is the supplication (الدعاء) following the prayer (الصلاة) while you are sitting.

And al-Qummi, narrated from him (peace be upon him), said: فَإِذا فَرَغْتَ ** (So when you are done) from your prophethood (نبوّتك), فَانْصَبْ ** (appoint) Ali, and ** وَإِلى رَبِّكَ فَارْغَبْ** (And turn to your Lord eagerly) in that matter.

In al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him) in a hadith, he said: He says: فَإِذا فَرَغْتَ فَانْصَبْ ** (So when you are done, appoint) your standard (علمك) and announce your successor (وصيّك), and inform them of his virtue publicly. So He said: "Of whomsoever I am the Mawla (مولاه), Ali is his Mawla (مولاه)"—the hadith. He said: That was when He informed him of his death and his soul was announced to him. And al-Qummi (said): فَإِذا فَرَغْتَ فَانْصَبْ ** (So when you are done, appoint) the Commander of the Faithful, Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him).

What is derived from these narratives is that it is read with a kasra on the ṣād (فَانْصِبْ) from the root النصب (with sukūn) meaning raising (الرّفع) and placing (الوضع). It means: So when you are done (فَإِذا فَرَغْتَ) with the matter of conveying the message (تبليغ الرسالة) and what is incumbent upon you to conclude regarding the laws (الشرايع) and rulings (الأحكام), فَانْصَبْ ** (appoint) your standard (علمك)—with a fatḥa on the lām—meaning: raise the standard of your guidance for the people and place the one who undertakes your succession (خلافتك) in your position, so that he may stand in your place after you by conveying the rulings (الأحكام) and guiding mankind (هداية الأنام), lest the thread of guidance and the message between Allah and His servants be severed. Rather, that should be continuous through the standing of an Imam in the place of an Imam forever until the Day of Resurrection. Al-Zamakhshari said in his Kashshāf: "And among the innovations (البِدَع) is what is narrated from some of the Rāfiḍah (الرافضة) that it was read فَانْصِبْ (with a kasra on the ṣād), meaning: Appoint (فَانْصِبْ) Ali (peace be upon him) for the Imamate (الإمامة)." He said: "If this were correct for the Rāfiḍī (الرّافضي), it would also be correct for the Nāṣibī (النّاصبي) to read it this way and make it an order for al-Naṣb (النصب), which is the hatred (بغض) and enmity (عداوته) of Ali (peace be upon him)."

I say: The appointment (نصب) of the Imam and the successor (الخليفة) after conveying the message (تبليغ الرسالة) or finishing the worship (العبادة) is a reasonable matter, rather, it is obligatory, lest the people fall into confusion (حيرة) and misguidance (ضلال) after him. Thus, it is correct for it to follow [the command]. As for the hatred (بغض) and enmity (عداوته) of Ali (peace be upon him), what is the reason for it to follow the conveyance of the message or the worship? And what is the reason for its reasonableness, considering that the books of the general populace (العامّة) are filled with mention of the love of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم for Ali (peace be upon him), and his public declaration of his virtue to the people throughout his life, and that loving him is faith (إيمان) and hating him is disbelief (كفر)? Look at this scholar (العلّامة) nicknamed Jār Allāh (جار الله), how Allah blinded his insight with the veil of zealous partisanship (حميّة التعصّب).

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Ayyāshī (العيّاشي) from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Do not combine two Surahs (سورتين) in one rak'ah (ركعة) except al-Ḍuḥā (الضّحى) and Alam Nashraḥ (أ لم نشرح), and Alam Tara Kayfa (وَأ لَمْ تَرَ كَيْفَ) and Li'īlāf Quraysh (وَلِايلاف قريش).
